title: Group-Aware Model Diversity and Rank Blending
summary: AUC rewards ordering, and previous runs rely on a single random split AutoGluon GBM/XGB family.
rationale: AUC rewards ordering, and previous runs rely on a single random split AutoGluon GBM/XGB family. For race strategy data, random row CV can overstate generalization if adjacent laps from the same race/driver appear in both train and validation.
implementation_hint: Train a small ensemble of diverse models: current AutoGluon GBM/XGB, CatBoost with native categorical features, and a shallow logistic or GAM-style model on hazard/window features. Validate with GroupKFold by Race-Year or leave-one-year/race groups, then blend predictions by rank averaging or weighted average chosen on grouped OOF AUC.
expected_effect: Could improve public robustness even if random local CV changes little, and rank averaging may stabilize probability scale differences without hurting ROC AUC.
risk: Grouped CV may score lower and be noisier; if Kaggle split is random-like, conservative blending might underperform the current high local CV model.
